data.html_notes:
<body>Goal
Accept a snapshot of all FE-derived wallet addresses and persist it temporarily for manual reconciliation and offline analysis (parent ticket RW-1807, requirement 3).

Specifications
- New endpoint in rumble-app-node for the authenticated wallet user to upload the FE address snapshot (all wallets / networks / tip jars: wallet name, wallet type, index, network, address).
- The snapshot is persisted in its own dedicated storage (separate collection), fully isolated from the wallets data.
- It must never overwrite or update the actual user wallet addresses. The backend remains the single source of truth for addresses. The snapshot is reference data for manual reconciliation only.
- Storage is temporary: kept for the duration of the reconciliation exercise, easy to export for offline analysis and to drop afterwards.

Why not reuse /api/v1/user-data
It was considered. It is safe with respect to the source of truth (it writes to the generic user-data KV collection, never to wallets), but it does not fit this use case:
- Client-owned semantics: latest-wins upsert plus a DELETE endpoint, so the client can replace or wipe the snapshot we want to analyze.
- Value is opaque JSON with no schema validation, no server timestamp, no app version.
- 64 KB value cap per key; users with many channel tip jars could approach it.
- Snapshots would be mixed into the generic user-data collection across shards under a magic key, which makes offline extraction harder than dumping a dedicated collection.
A dedicated endpoint plus collection keeps the snapshot immutable, validated, timestamped and trivially exportable, and follows the same app-node to ork to data-shard path so the effort is small.</body>
data.name: BE API for Address snapshot
data.notes:
